chapter_title: Man-made filaments; strip and the like of man-made textile materials
scope_note: Note 1: 'man-made fibres' = organic polymer fibres produced by: (a) SYNTHETIC: polymerisation of organic monomers (polyamides, polyesters, polyolefins, polyurethanes, PVA etc.) OR chemical modification thereof; (b) ARTIFICIAL: dissolution/chemical treatment of natural organic polymers (cellulose → viscose/cupro, cellulose acetate, alginates, casein etc.). Strip and the like of 54.04/54.05 are NOT man-made fibres. Note 2: 54.02/54.03 do NOT apply to filament tow of Ch.55.
inclusions: Heading 54.01 — Sewing thread of man-made filaments, whether or not put up for retail sale

Heading 54.02 — Synthetic filament yarn (other than sewing thread), not put up for retail sale, including synthetic monofilament of less than 67 decitex: high-tenacity yarn of nylon or other polyamides; high-tenacity yarn of polyesters; textured yarn (polyamide, polyester, polypropylene); other yarn (single, multiple or cabled). Note 2: excludes synthetic filament tow of Ch.55

Heading 54.03 — Artificial filament yarn (other than sewing thread), not put up for retail sale, including artificial monofilament of less than 67 decitex: high-tenacity viscose yarn; textured viscose yarn; other artificial yarn (viscose, cupro, modal, lyocell, acetate). Note 2: excludes artificial filament tow of Ch.55

Heading 54.04 — Synthetic monofilament of 67 decitex or more and of which no cross-sectional dimension exceeds 1 mm; strip and the like (for example, artificial straw) of synthetic textile materials of an apparent width not exceeding 5 mm

Heading 54.05 — Artificial monofilament of 67 decitex or more and of which no cross-sectional dimension exceeds 1 mm; strip and the like of artificial textile materials of an apparent width not exceeding 5 mm
Heading 54.06 — Man-made filament yarn (other than sewing thread), put up for retail sale
Heading 54.07 — Woven fabrics of synthetic filament yarn, including woven fabrics obtained from materials of heading 54.04 (e.g., woven fabrics of strips)
Heading 54.08 — Woven fabrics of artificial filament yarn, including woven fabrics obtained from materials of heading 54.05

exclusions.condition: Synthetic or artificial filament tow (multiple parallel filaments forming a bundle) of Chapter 55
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 55 (headings 55.01/55.02) — Note 2. Filament tow meeting Ch.55 Note 1 specs (>2m, <5 turns/m, <67 dtex/filament, drawn for synthetic, total >20,000 dtex) → Ch.55. 54.02/54.03 exclude tow

exclusions.condition: Man-made monofilament of 67 decitex or more, or strip/tape wider than 5 mm
exclusions.action: Monofilament ≥67 dtex → 54.04 (synthetic) or 54.05 (artificial) if cross-section ≤1mm. Strip/tape wider than 5mm apparent width → NOT Ch.54 strip; classified by plastic or material chapter. Cross-section >1mm → classified as plastic rod/tube/strip → Ch.39

exclusions.condition: Woven fabrics of man-made filaments that are impregnated, coated, covered or laminated
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 59 (coated/laminated textile fabrics). PU-coated polyester filament fabric → 59.03. Rubberised nylon fabric → 59.06

exclusions.condition: Knitted or crocheted fabrics of man-made filament yarn
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 60 (knitted or crocheted fabrics). Man-made filament knit fabric → Ch.60. Made-up knitted garments → Ch.61

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Synthetic vs Artificial distinction: the foundational definition for Section XI
classification_rules.description: The SYNTHETIC / ARTIFICIAL split is the fundamental classification divide for man-made fibres throughout Section XI: SYNTHETIC = produced by POLYMERISATION of organic monomers (polyamide/nylon, polyester/PET, polyolefin/PP/PE, polyacrylic/PAN, polyurethane, PVA). ARTIFICIAL = produced by DISSOLUTION or CHEMICAL TREATMENT of natural organic polymers (cellulose → viscose rayon, cuprammonium rayon/cupro, modal, lyocell; cellulose acetate; alginates). The source polymer (synthetic from petrochemicals vs. artificial from natural cellulose/protein) determines which half of all man-made textile headings applies.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.54 (FILAMENT — continuous) vs Ch.55 (STAPLE — cut/broken)
classification_rules.description: The primary Ch.54 vs Ch.55 distinction: Ch.54 = CONTINUOUS FILAMENT man-made fibres (not cut or broken — monofilament, multifilament yarn, tow if <2m but mainly yarn). Ch.55 = STAPLE and STAPLE-DERIVED (filaments cut to short discrete lengths). Exception: Note 2 — filament TOW (multiple parallel filaments as a bundle) meeting Ch.55 Note 1 specs → Ch.55 (55.01/55.02), NOT Ch.54. This means large-scale tow bundles for downstream cutting go to Ch.55 despite being long continuous filaments.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 54.04/54.05 (monofilament ≥67 dtex or strip ≤5mm) vs 54.02/54.03 (fine multifilament yarn <67 dtex) vs Ch.39 (plastic rod/tape)
classification_rules.description: Three thresholds matter: (1) FINENESS: monofilament <67 decitex → yarn heading (54.02/54.03). Monofilament ≥67 dtex → 54.04 (synthetic) or 54.05 (artificial) IF cross-section ≤1mm. (2) CROSS-SECTION: if any dimension >1mm → no longer textile monofilament → plastic/artificial rod → Ch.39. (3) STRIP: flat tape/strip of apparent width ≤5mm → 54.04/54.05. Width >5mm → Ch.39 (plastics strip). These thresholds separate fine textile filaments from plastic profile extrusions.

classification_rules.rule: Retail sale vs. industrial presentation: 54.06 vs 54.02/54.03
classification_rules.description: Man-made filament yarn headings split by PRESENTATION/INTENDED BUYER: 54.01 = sewing thread (both synthetic and artificial, retail or not). 54.02 = synthetic filament yarn NOT for retail (large industrial cones, cheeses for textile manufacturing). 54.03 = artificial filament yarn NOT for retail (industrial packages). 54.06 = man-made filament yarn (both synthetic and artificial) PUT UP FOR RETAIL SALE (small balls/skeins/bobbins for home knitting/crocheting). The retail packaging (small quantities, consumer presentation) determines 54.06 vs. 54.02/54.03.

classification_rules.rule: Woven fabric headings: 54.07 (synthetic filament) vs 54.08 (artificial filament) — includes strip-woven fabrics
classification_rules.description: Two woven fabric headings by filament type: 54.07 = woven fabrics of SYNTHETIC filament yarn OR of 54.04 strip materials (polyester wovens, nylon wovens, polypropylene strip fabrics, PP tape-woven bags base fabric). 54.08 = woven fabrics of ARTIFICIAL filament yarn or 54.05 strip materials (viscose georgette, rayon satin, acetate lining fabrics). When mixed synthetic/artificial → classify by predominant fibre. When coated → Ch.59. When knitted → Ch.60.
